category_title: Students with EBD experiencing behavioral outbursts and reading difficulties in science
scenario_title: Memorizing science vocabulary and facts during peer tutoring
minimal_context: Sam is working in a peer group using an academic task checklist and a letter mnemonic to memorize vocabulary words about the characteristics of matter.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at your academic task checklist and use the letter mnemonic to study the vocabulary words for the key characteristics of matter.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't want to read these words. The time I spent out of class has left me too far behind.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think the first word says? Just try your best.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't know! I struggle with reading and I can't figure out what this mnemonic means.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Keep trying to sound it out so we can finish the checklist.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Pushes the academic task checklist off the desk and shouts) I said I'm not reading it! I'm not doing this stupid science work anymore!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's look at your academic task checklist and use the letter mnemonic to study the vocabulary words for the key characteristics of matter.

STUDENT: I don't want to read these words. The time I spent out of class has left me too far behind.

TUTOR: What do you think the first word says? Just try your best.

STUDENT: I don't know! I struggle with reading and I can't figure out what this mnemonic means.

TUTOR: Keep trying to sound it out so we can finish the checklist.

STUDENT: (Pushes the academic task checklist off the desk and shouts) I said I'm not reading it! I'm not doing this stupid science work anymore!
